# Member Data Access Gap Memo Template

Last reviewed: 2026-04-21

Use this when a society's bylaws, funding model, or institutional relationship
suggest a member list should exist, but a university, student union, parent
body, funder, or other institution does not provide member-level information.

This is a governance and privacy documentation template, not legal advice.

## Memo

### 1. Organization

- Legal name: [legal name]
- Incorporation number: [if applicable]
- Date prepared: [YYYY-MM-DD]
- Prepared by: [name / role]
- Reviewed by: [board / officer / privacy officer / counsel]
- Next review date: [YYYY-MM-DD]

### 2. Issue

[Organization] does not currently have a complete member-level list from
[institution / parent body / collection agent]. This memo records what data the
organization has, what data it requested, what limits apply, and how the
organization will continue to comply with privacy and society-record duties.

### 3. Membership Basis

Membership is defined by:

- Constitution/bylaws section: [quote or summarize]
- Statute or institutional rule, if any: [quote or summarize]
- Funding or fee arrangement, if relevant: [quote or summarize]

Current understanding:

[Explain who is believed to be a member, eligible member, fee-paying student,
subscriber, volunteer, staff member, or other relevant class.]

### 4. Records the Organization Is Expected to Maintain

Relevant records may include:

- society register of members;
- member names and contact information provided by members;
- class of membership, if applicable;
- voting eligibility;
- AGM/election/referendum notice lists;
- consent and communication preferences;
- fee/remittance records;
- event or program participation records; and
- records required for grants, employment, payroll, tax, insurance, or legal
  compliance.

Specific records needed:

[List the records needed and why.]

### 5. Institution or Parent Body Holding Data

- Institution/body name: [name]
- Contact person/office: [name / office / email]
- Relationship to organization: [university / student union / collection agent /
  parent body / funder / service provider / other]
- Type of data believed to be held: [member names, emails, fee status, program,
  student number, class, eligibility, aggregate counts, aggregate remittances,
  etc.]

### 6. Data Requested

Requested fields:

- [field]
- [field]
- [field]

Requested purpose:

[Examples: maintaining the member register, AGM notice, elections, referenda,
services eligibility, communication with members, reconciling fees, statutory
compliance.]

Date requested: [YYYY-MM-DD]

Method: [email / meeting / form / agreement request / other]

Request record stored at: [document link / email archive / Paperless ID /
Societyer document]

### 7. Institution Response

Response date: [YYYY-MM-DD]

Response summary:

[Summarize refusal, partial disclosure, aggregate-only disclosure, required
agreement, required consent, or no response.]

Evidence:

- [document link / Paperless ID / email reference]
- [document link / Paperless ID / email reference]

### 8. Custody and Control Analysis

The organization currently treats the institution-held list as outside the
organization's custody and control because:

- the organization does not possess a copy;
- the organization does not have login access to the system;
- the institution has not agreed to provide the data;
- there is no current service-provider relationship giving the organization
  control over the data;
- the organization cannot direct the institution's use, disclosure, retention,
  or correction of the data; and
- [other facts].

If any of these facts change, this memo must be reviewed.

### 9. Data the Organization Does Control

The organization does control these records:

- directors/officers: [system/location]
- staff/contractors: [system/location]
- volunteers: [system/location]
- direct member signups: [system/location]
- mailing list subscribers: [system/location]
- event registrations: [system/location]
- meeting attendance and proxy records: [system/location]
- election/referendum records received by the organization: [system/location]
- grant, reimbursement, payment, or payroll records: [system/location]
- complaints/access requests: [system/location]
- cloud drives, email accounts, Paperless, Societyer, or other systems:
  [system/location]

For these records, the organization remains responsible for PIPA compliance.

### 10. Mitigation Plan

The organization will:

- maintain the member records it can lawfully maintain;
- collect information directly from members where appropriate, with notice and
  consent or other lawful authority;
- keep aggregate remittance records separate from member-level records;
- avoid scraping, buying, or inferring substitute member lists without legal
  authority;
- segregate any referendum-only or purpose-limited data;
- restrict access to personal information by role;
- keep correspondence with the institution;
- request a lawful data-sharing agreement if needed;
- review AGM, election, referendum, and member-notice risks before each cycle;
  and
- seek legal advice where the gap affects statutory compliance or member rights.

### 11. Board Position

Recommended wording:

> The Society requested access to the institutional member list for the purpose
> of maintaining society records and communicating with members. As of [date],
> [institution] has not disclosed that list and has not provided the Society
> with access to or control over those records. The Society therefore treats the
> institutional list as outside the Society's custody and control for PIPA
> response purposes, while continuing to comply with PIPA for personal
> information the Society collects or controls and continuing to maintain the
> member records lawfully available to it.

For statutory referendum or fee-process data:

> Any student personal information received for a statutory referendum or fee
> process will be segregated, used only for that purpose, access-restricted, and
> deleted or retained only as required for legal, audit, or dispute-resolution
> purposes.

### 12. Approvals and Review

- Approved by: [board / executive / officer]
- Approval date: [YYYY-MM-DD]
- Privacy officer: [name / role]
- Next review trigger: [annual review / AGM / election / referendum / new
  agreement / institution response]

## Evidence Checklist

- [ ] Current bylaws or membership definition attached.
- [ ] Institution request attached.
- [ ] Institution response or no-response record attached.
- [ ] Data inventory attached.
- [ ] Direct collection plan documented.
- [ ] Referendum/fee-purpose restrictions documented, if applicable.
- [ ] Board approval recorded.
- [ ] Next review date set.
